Since we want the shortest distance to a gate, use BFS. Start a multi-source BFS from all gates simultaneously.
Initialize a queue with the coordinates of all gates (0). Set their distance to 0.
Pop from the queue and check adjacent cells. If an adjacent cell is empty (INF), update its value to the current distance + 1 and push to the queue.
Since BFS processes level by level, the first time you reach an empty cell, that distance is guaranteed to be the shortest.
